user_query:
I’m coordinating a short Holsted Tigers volunteers meeting before the next home match and need your help to pick the time and prepare the comms and materials from our local files. Please work only with the files in input/ and produce the following outputs under output/.

Inputs:
- input/availability.csv (RSVPs for three proposed slots)
- input/minutes_last_meeting.md (minutes with [OPEN] and [DONE] items)
- input/draft_invite.txt (rough invite to rewrite)

What to do and expected outputs:
1) Compute slot availability stats and choose the meeting time:
   - For each slot (the date/time column headers in input/availability.csv), count how many responses are Available, Maybe, and No. Compute a weighted score using Available=1, Maybe=0.5, No or missing=0.
   - Select the slot with the highest weighted score. Break ties by the earlier date/time (the headers are in YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM, so lexical sort equals chronological).
   - Save a CSV to output/slot_stats.csv with columns exactly: slot,available_count,maybe_count,no_count,weighted_score. Include all three slots, sorted by weighted_score descending, then by slot ascending.

2) Rewrite the meeting invite for tone, clarity, and brevity:
   - Use input/draft_invite.txt as the source. Preserve the first line subject text exactly as-is at the top of the output.
   - Rewrite the body into a friendly, concise Holsted Tigers–spirited invitation of at most 120 words that:
     • States the selected slot string exactly as it appears in the CSV header.
     • States the location as: Clubhouse meeting room (use this exact phrasing).
     • Mentions the purpose (match day roles and materials).
     • Asks for RSVP by 2026-04-17.
   - Save to output/meeting_invite.txt.

3) Prepare a combined agenda and carry-over action items document:
   - From input/minutes_last_meeting.md, extract only lines that begin with “- [OPEN]”. For each, keep the description, Owner, and Due if present.
   - Create output/agenda_and_actions.md containing:
     • Title: “Volunteer Coordination Meeting — Holsted Tigers”.
     • When: <selected slot string>
     • Where: Clubhouse meeting room
     • Agenda: 4–6 bullets (include: Volunteer roles, Materials checklist, Stadium coordination, Open items from last meeting, and 1–2 other relevant items).
     • Carry-over action items: bullets normalized as “- [ ] <Description> (Owner: <Name>; Due: <YYYY-MM-DD>)”. Include all [OPEN] items from the minutes in this normalized form.

4) Compose a short status summary for the committee:
   - In output/status_summary.md, include:
     • A brief paragraph summarizing the process and the selected slot.
     • A bulleted list of the three slots with their available_count, maybe_count, no_count, and weighted_score (pull from your computed stats).
     • The tie-break rationale (earlier date/time if applicable).
     • The count of carry-over [OPEN] action items you included.

Notes and constraints:
- Use only the provided inputs; no Internet or external data.
- When referencing the meeting time in any output, use the slot string exactly as it appears in input/availability.csv.
- All outputs must be saved to the specified paths under output/ so they can be verified. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
